description: Used only for morphological errors involving the incorrect use of derivational affixes.
question: 이 단어 모양이 맞나요? 처음이나 끝부분을 바꿔야 하나요?

description: Includes all spelling errors, such as capitalization, consonant or vowel doubling, letter substitution, hyphen misuse, homophone confusion, and simple typos.
question: 철자가 맞나요? (또는 대소문자, 띄어쓰기도 확인해보세요.)

description: Includes all cases where the use of the definite article, indefinite article, or zero article violates general grammatical rules.
question: 이 명사는 ‘하나’인가요, ‘그것(특정한 것)’인가요? a/an/the 중 뭐가 맞나요?
Example sentences: I’d like **a** pizza. | I’d like **an** orange juice. | There is **a** mirror. | Look at **the** sky. | Let’s open **the** door.

description: Used for the incorrect formation or misuse of comparative and superlative forms.
question: ‘더 ~’, ‘가장 ~’를 말하려고 하나요? 모양이 맞나요?
Example sentences: Your drone is **bigger than** mine. | I’m **taller than** you. | The zebra is **older than** the pig. | I’m going to join **the best** soccer team in Argentina. | What’s your **favorite** subject?

description: Includes number agreement errors involving the incorrect addition of a plural ending to an adjective.
question: 형용사 모양이 문장에 맞나요?
Example sentences: Four **small shoes** are on the box. | She has eight **long legs**. | The **colorful trees** are so beautiful. | I like to read **history books**. | I like to watch **English movies**.

description: Used for errors in adjective position, such as placing an adjective after a noun or using the wrong order of multiple adjectives.
question: 형용사를 여러 개 썼다면 순서가 자연스럽나요?
Example sentences: She has **long straight** hair. | It’s a **beautiful sunny** day. | You’re **very kind**. | He has **short curly hair**. | They’re **too expensive**.

description: Indicates cases where an adverb is placed incorrectly, such as between a verb and its object.
question: 이 말(부사)이 들어갈 자리가 맞나요?
Example sentences: He can play basketball **very well.**

description: Includes all errors related to demonstrative determiners such as this and these.
question: 이 말(this/that/these/those)은 무엇을 가리키나요?
Example sentences: **This** egg sandwich is so delicious! | Look at **that** big fish. | How much are **these** pants? | Mom, look at **those** cute gloves. | Look at **those** colorful boxes!

description: Includes all errors related to indefinite determiners such as all, another, any, each, and some.
question: 이 말(some/any/many/much/a few 등)은 양이나 수에 맞나요?
Example sentences: Here’s **some** paper. | Do you want **some** juice? | Do you have **any** crayons? | Did you see **any** animals? | I will read **many** books.

description: Includes all errors related to possessive determiners such as my and your.
question: 이 말(my/your/his/her/our/their)은 누구의 것인가요?
Example sentences: **My** name is Tom. | What’s **your** name? | Science is **his** favorite subject. | I’m waiting for **my** sister. I have **her** umbrella. | Today, let’s talk about **our** favorite subjects. | Dogs are looking for **their** toys.

description: Includes all other determiner errors, including relative and interrogative determiners.
question: 이 말(each/every/another 등)은 뜻에 맞나요?
Example sentences: I exercise **every** day. | We play badminton at school **every** morning. | I play soccer with my dad **every** evening. | I eat fresh fruits and vegetables **every** day.

description: Used for the incorrect use or omission of the possessive ’s, or for using another structure where the possessive form is required.
question: ‘~의’(’s)처럼 표시가 꼭 필요한가요? 없어도 되나요?
Example sentences: It's **Inho’s**. | It is my **grandpa’s** hat. | We’ll visit the **art center**. | I’m going to take a **guitar lesson**. | Welcome to the **Animals’ Sports** Day. | I’m going to write my name on my **friends’ T-shirt**.

description: Indicates noun number errors caused by adding or omitting a plural ending.
question: 하나인가요 여러 개인가요? -s를 붙여야 하나요?
Example sentences: Whose **glasses** are these? | Four small **shoes** are on the box. | There are two **chairs**. | Whose **socks** are these? | Put on your **socks** and **shoes**, please.

description: Includes all errors related to the use of demonstrative pronouns such as this and that.
question: 이 말(this/that/these/those)이 무엇을 가리키는지 분명한가요?
Example sentences: What’s **this**? It’s a ball. | **This** is a tiger. | **That’s** the first airplane. | What are **these**? **They’re** buttons. | What are **those**? **They’re** maps.

description: Includes errors related to reflexive pronouns ending in -self and reciprocal pronouns such as each other and one another.
question: myself/each other 같은 말이 필요한가요? 모양이 맞나요?
Example sentences: Help **yourself**. | You can see **yourself** and the city around you.

description: Includes all errors involving indefinite pronouns such as some, any, everyone, and nothing, as well as the substitute forms one, ones, and so.
question: someone/anything 같은 말이 뜻에 맞나요?
Example sentences: Hello, **everyone**. | In the end, **everyone** is safe. | Is there **anything** fun? | I want to buy **something** for my brother. | The tree gives **everything** to a boy.

description: Includes all errors related to the use of possessive pronouns such as mine, yours, and theirs.
question: mine/yours 같은 말이 필요한가요? 아니면 my/your가 맞나요?
Example sentences: It's **mine**. | Is this **yours**? | Yes, it’s **mine**. | **Mine** is green. | It’s not **mine**.

description: Applies to all personal pronoun errors, including errors involving impersonal pronouns such as it and there.
question: I/me/he/him 같은 말이 자리(앞/뒤)에 맞나요?
Example sentences: Where is Lucas? I can’t find **him**. | **She’s** **my** sister, Kate. ... Oh! **She’s** tall. | Look at **them**! Cows and ducks. | There are three dogs. May I touch **them**? | Can **you** tell **us** about **your** day?

description: Includes all errors related to relative pronouns and interrogative pronouns.
question: who/which/what 같은 말이 여기서 맞나요?
Example sentences: **What** time do you get up? | **What** time do you go home? | **What** time do you have breakfast?

description: Used when the referent of a pronoun is not clear from the context.
question: it/this/they가 무엇을 가리키는지 헷갈리지 않나요?
Example sentences: Look at the tree. **It’s** very tall. | I caught a big fish and cooked **it** for dinner. | What are **these**? **They’re** buttons. | Whose glasses are **these**? **They’re** Yuna’s. | **This** is my dad.

description: Includes the misuse of primary auxiliaries such as be, have, and do, as well as modal and semi-modal auxiliaries.
question: do/be/have/can 같은 말이 필요하나요? 모양이 맞나요?
Example sentences: I **can** play the violin. | You **can** put your things in the basket. | **Do** you like pizza? | **Don’t** eat, please. | Everybody **doesn't** look the same.

description: Indicates verb form errors related to inflection, such as using a past form instead of a past participle.
question: 동사 끝부분(-s/-ed/-ing)이 맞나요?
Example sentences: I’m **making** a piano. | My bird is **flying**. | He’s **playing** the janggu. | I’m **looking** for my brother. | It’s **raining** outside.

description: Used for number agreement errors between the subject and the verb.
question: 주어가 하나인가요? 동사에 -s가 필요하나요?
Example sentences: I **have** many friends in my class. | He **lives** in Korea now. | We **have** many classes today. | I **work** with farm animals. | It **is** very nice.

description: Used for errors in choosing a finite or non-finite verb form, such as using -ing instead of a relative clause.
question: to ~ / ~ing 중 무엇이 맞나요?
Example sentences: I like **to make** new things. | I like **to play** the piano. | I like **to read** books. | I love **dancing** very much. | I like **singing** songs.

description: Used for the incorrect use of tense or aspect.
question: 이 일은 언제 일어난 일인가요? 과거/지금/앞으로 중 뭐가 맞나요?
Example sentences: I **went** on a trip. | I **visited** my grandfather. | I **ate** delicious food. | I **took** a walk on Ollegil. | We **bought** a beautiful dress.  | | **I’ll visit** my cousins. | I **will join** a space camp. | I **will make** a robot. | I **will learn** Spanish. | / **I'll be** a gardener in San Diego.

description: Includes all errors involving confusion between active and passive voice.
question: ‘누가 했다’인가요, ‘~당했다/되었다’인가요?
Example sentences: Van Gogh **painted** it. | Yi Sunsin **built** the ship. | Shakespeare **wrote** the story. | It **is made** with Hanji. | **I’m interested** in history.

description: Used for errors caused by confusion between word classes, such as using an adjective instead of a noun.
question: 품사를 헷갈리지는 않았나요? (사물을 나타내는 말, 사물을 꾸미는 말, 행동과 관련된 말을 꾸미는 말 등)
Example sentences: The tiger cried **sadly**. | Listen **carefully**. | This is a present for **healthy** children.

description: Indicates errors in adjective complement structure, such as using the wrong infinitive or gerund form after an adjective.
question: 이 형용사 뒤에 오는 말(to/that/명사 등)이 맞나요?
Example sentences: **Nice to** meet you. | **Good to** see you again.

description: Used for errors in choosing the preposition that depends on a specific adjective.
question: 이 형용사 뒤 전치사(in/on/at 등)가 맞나요?
Example sentences: I’m **interested in** history. | I’m **interested in** science. | I’m **interested in** art.

description: Used for errors in choosing the preposition that depends on a specific adverb.
question: 이 부사 뒤 전치사(in/on/at 등)가 맞나요?
Example sentences: It’s **next to** the toy shop. | It’s **next to** the bookstore. | Turn **right at** the corner. | Turn **left at** the bus stop.

description: Indicates errors in noun complement structure, such as using the wrong complement pattern after a noun.
question: 이 명사 뒤에 오는 말(of/to/that 등)이 맞나요?
Example sentences: It’s **time to** go home. | It's a **day for** reading.

description: Used for errors in choosing the preposition that depends on a specific noun.
question: 이 명사 뒤 전치사(of/in/on 등)가 맞나요?
Example sentences: It’s **time for** breakfast. | I have no **ideas for** the school festival. | It’s a **book about** stars.

description: Indicates countability errors, such as using a/an with an uncountable noun or adding a plural ending to it.
question: 이 명사는 셀 수 있나요? a/an, -s가 필요한가요?
Example sentences: I want **some water**. | I want **some milk**. | I like **apples**.

description: Indicates errors in prepositional complement structure, such as using the wrong form after a preposition.
question: 이 전치사 뒤에 오는 말(명사/~ing 등)이 맞나요?
Example sentences: Thanks **for helping** me. | Thanks **for joining** Willy’s quiz show. | How **about riding** the bikes together? |  I’m good **at playing** soccer. | I’m interested **in baking**.

description: Indicates errors in verb complement structure, such as using the wrong infinitive or gerund pattern required by a verb.
question: 이 동사 뒤에 오는 말(to/~ing/that 등)이 맞나요?
Example sentences: I **want to** be a pilot. | **I’m going to** bake cookies. | I **like making** robots. | I’ll **go fishing** with my dad.

description: Used for errors in choosing the preposition that depends on a specific verb, including omission or unnecessary addition of a preposition.
question: 이 동사 뒤 전치사(to/on/for 등)가 맞나요?
Example sentences: I'm **listening to** music. | **Look at** this picture. | I **learned about** trees.

description: This is a lexical connector error involving the incorrect choice, unnecessary use, or omission of a coordinating conjunction such as and, but, or, or nor.
question: and/but/or 중 어떤 게 뜻에 맞나요?
Example sentences: I like fruit salad **and** pizza. | Science is fun, **but** it’s not my favorite. | He likes bulgogi **and** tteokbokki. | Art, music, English, **and** science. | I will play **and** plant trees with my friends.

description: This is a lexical connector error involving a multi-word phrasal connector, such as using On other side instead of On the other hand.
question: on the other hand/in addition 같은 표현이 뜻에 맞나요?

description: This is a lexical connector error involving a one-word conjunctive adverb used to express a logical relation between sentences, such as misusing however, therefore, moreover, or so.
question: however/therefore 같은 말이 뜻에 맞나요?
Example sentences: Science is fun, **but** it’s not my favorite | **Also**, we can see colorful trees. | **Then**, I go home at two o’clock. | **So**, I’m helping Rosetta.

description: This is a lexical connector error involving the incorrect choice, misuse, or omission of a subordinating conjunction such as because, although, if, or that.
question: because/when/if 같은 말이 필요한가요?
Example sentences: I like spring **because** I can see the beautiful flowers. | I’m angry **because** my brother broke my robot. | I’m worried **because** I have a math quiz. | I’m very happy **because** I’m going to see a soccer game.

description: This is a lexical error involving a fixed word combination, such as an idiom, multi-word unit, or phrasal verb, where the meaning or form of the whole expression is incorrect.
question: 이 표현이 영어에서 자주 쓰이는 말인가요? 더 자연스러운 말이 있나요?

description: This is a single-word lexical error involving the incorrect choice of an adjective or an inappropriate collocation in context.
question: 이 말(형용사)이 뜻에 맞나요?

description: This is a single-word lexical error involving the incorrect choice or conceptual use of an adverb in context.
question: 이 말(부사)이 뜻에 맞나요?

description: This is a single-word lexical error involving the incorrect conceptual choice of a noun or the use of an inappropriate noun in context.
question: 이 말(명사)이 뜻에 맞나요?

description: This is a single-word lexical error involving the incorrect choice of an independent preposition, such as one used for time or place.
question: 이 말(전치사)이 관계(장소/시간/방향)에 맞나요?

description: This is a single-word lexical error involving the incorrect semantic choice of a verb or the use of an unnatural verb combination.
question: 이 말(동사)이 뜻에 맞나요?

description: Indicates confusion between punctuation marks, such as using a period where a comma is needed or using a comma where a period is needed.
question: 여기에 . , ? ! 중 어떤 게 맞나요?

description: Indicates the use of a lexical item, usually a coordinating conjunction, where punctuation is needed, or the use of punctuation where a lexical item is needed.
question: 여기서는 쉼표/마침표를 써야 하나요, 아니면 and 같은 말을 써야 하나요?

description: Indicates the omission of punctuation required by the context, such as a missing comma.
question: 여기에 구두점이 빠졌나요?

description: Indicates the unnecessary use of punctuation.
question: 여기 구두점이 너무 많나요?

description: Indicates that a word has been omitted when no more specific grammatical tag applies.
question: 빠진 말이 있나요?

description: Indicates incorrect word order required by sentence structure, including question inversion, excluding errors involving only the position of an adjective or adverb.
question: 단어 순서가 자연스럽나요?
Example sentences: **Where** is my phone? | **How** old are you? | **Don’t** run, please.

description: Indicates that one or more unnecessary words are included.
question: 불필요한 단어가 있나요? 빼도 뜻이 같나요?

description: Indicates an expression that is not clearly wrong but is awkward or inappropriate in style, register, or usage.
question: 틀린 건 아니어도, 이 말이 자연스럽나요? 더 흔한 말이 있나요?

description: Indicates an error in Korean script.
question: 이 단어를 뜻하는 영어 단어는 무엇일까요?
